entities.name: Founder Dinner
entities.context: Gathering where peers agreed they screen more than ever yet are no more confident about who to interview

entities.name: Top-of-Funnel Signal Problem
entities.context: Shared problem statement: more volume without better signal is actively harmful to founders

entities.name: Self-Reflection UX
entities.context: Core Careerspan experience—longer-form, coached conversations about specific work stories

entities.name: Structured Evidence Model
entities.context: System that decomposes roles into responsibilities and skills, then maps candidate stories onto that structure

entities.name: Role Decomposition
entities.context: Breaking roles into responsibilities and skills so candidate fit and gaps become visible to employers

relationships.context: V is the founder of Careerspan
V crystallised this as the shared problem at the founder dinner
Careerspan exists to solve the signal-over-volume problem for hiring
Coffee Space is also trying to solve the same screening-signal gap
Core product experience is coached self-reflection conversations
System turns work stories into structured evidence mapped to role requirements
Evidence model depends on decomposing roles into responsibilities and skills
Both companies address the same founder hiring-signal problem from different angles
V attended and recounted the moment where the shared problem was articulated
